MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-02-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED IN THEIR ENTIRETY. THERE IS A TRIABLE DISPUTE REGARDING THE AMOUNT OF FEES OWED BY DEFENDANT SHERIF REZKALLA. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT IT IS ENTITLED AS A MATTER OF LAW TO ALL OF THE FEES THAT ARE INCLUDED IN THE AMOUNT IT SEEKS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189 =302/HK

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2020 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-21-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-02-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff American Express Centurion Bank's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied in their entirety. There is a triable dispute regarding the amount of fees owed by defendant Sherif Rezkalla. Ms. Rezkalla presented evidence disputing the amount of fees that are included in the amount sought by the plaintiff. (Rezkalla Dec. par. 10). Plaintiff has not shown that it is entitled as a matter of law to all of the fees that are included in the amount it seeks. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
